California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
December 3■
, ■990
Ross G. Hubbard′  Town Manager
2■00 Dona■d Dr■ve
p.0. Box 
■88
MOraga′  CA  94556
Re:  Your Request for Adv■
ce
Our Fi■e No. I-90-700
Dear Mr. Hubbard:
This is in response to your November 21 , L99o, written
request for advice concerning-the nass uailing provisions of the
Potitical Reform Act (t'Acttr):1 
Because your letter lacks sorne
facts (discussed below) necessary for us to fu11y anslrer your
question, we provide you with only informal advice. (Regulation
La329 , copy enclosed. )
OUESTTON
May the Town of ltoraga list the names of its councilmembers
in j.ts iecreation brochure and what you refer to as trother sj.milar
publications,rr along srith the nanes of the park and recreation
commissioner and staff nembers?
CONCI,USION
We lack sufficient facts to say with certainty that the
publications at issue falI within the definition of mass mailing.
If we assume they do, the town would be permitted to include in
them the nanes of its councilmembers, along with those of the park
and recreation commissioner and staff members -- provided that (1,)
the councilmembers' names appear in a roster listing, 
(2) the
Iisting contains the narnes of all elected officers of the town,
and (3) their names are printed in the same type size,. typeface,
type color and locat j-on.
1 
co\r"..,ment code Sections 81ooo-9L015. AtI statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwi,se indicated.
comrnission regulations appear at 2 California code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. AIl references to regrulations are to
Title 2. Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
428 J Street,Suite 800 ● P,0.Box 807 0 Sacramento CA 95804・0807 ● (916)322・5660


Our File No.  I-90-700
Page 2
FACTS
The Torrn of Moraga routinely maiLs two publications to 6,000
households within the town. one is a newsletter; the other a
recreation brochure. They are prepared by the toirn staff without
t'direct inputtt from the town council.
rt appears from your letter that the town also publishes, or
is considering pubtishing, other publications sj-rnilar to the
newsletter, recreation brochure, or both.
ANALYSTS
Section 89001 says, rrNo newsletter or other mass nailing
shall be sent at public expense. rr Subdivision (a) of Regulation
18901 (copy enclosed) defines rrrnass mailingrr for purposes of
Section 89001. Under that subdivision, an itern will be considered
a mass nailing if all of these criteria are met:
(1) [The] iten sent is delivered, by any
neans, to the recipient at his or her
residence, pLace of enplolment or business, or
post office box....
(2) The iten sent either:
(A) Features an elected officer
affiliated 
with the agency which produces or
sends the mailing, or
(B) Includes the name, office,
photograph, or other reference to an elected
officer affiliated with the agency whiclt
produces or sends the nailing, and is prepared
or sent in cooperation, consultation, co-
ordination, or concert with the elected
officer.
(3) (A) Any of the costs of distribution
[are] paid for with public moneys; or
(B) costs of design, product j,on, and
printing exceeding $50.00 are paid with public
noneys, and the design, production, or
printing is done rrith the intent-of sending
the item other than as permitted by this
regulation.


Our File No.
Page 3
I-90-700
(4) More than tr,ro hundred substantially
sinilar items are sent, j-n a singl-e calendar
month....
In this case, hre knov, that rnore than 2o0 copies of the same
recreation brochure are mail,ed to residences at public expense.
If such brochures include the names or other references to the
councilmember, the criteria for a mass mailing have been met.2
Ho$rever, even if we assume that all these criteria have been
met and that the recreation brochure and other sinilar
publications therefore constitute mass nailinE s, your proposal to
include the names of the councilmembers would stil1 be permissi,ble
if certain requirernents, set forth in Subdivision (b) (1) of
Regulation 189o1-, are Det. In your situation, the applicable
re{uirements are that (1) the counci Imembers' names appear in a
roiter listing, 
(2) the listing contaj,ns the names of alL elected
officers of the torrn, and (3) their names are printed in the same
type size, typeface, type color and location.
I hope this resPonds to
questions, feel free to caIl your inquiry. 
If you have any other
ne at (916) 322-59oL.
Sincere Iy,
scott Hallabrin
Acting General- counsel
By: Steven R. Rhoads
counsel. Legal Division
SH: SR: d9
Enc Iosures
2 Yo, say in your letter that the brochures have been prepared
without trdirect input" fron members of the town council. If the
brochure is produced conpletely independent of the councilmembers,
names of councilmenbers may be included in the brochure so long as
they are not rrfeatured,rr (Regulation 18901(a) (2) (A) and (c) (2);
Smook]er Advice Letter A'89-374, Welihan Advice Letter I-89-63,
and Bulens Advice Letter A-89-215, copies enclosed.)
